plumber.name: Pimlico Plumbers
plumber.standout: 45-year heritage timeline, 24/7 messaging, interactive London borough coverage map, and massive trust signal wall (Gas Safe, NICEIC, CHAS, Trustpilot) — the gold standard for a large trades company site
plumber.key_element: Interactive service selector dropdown ('I need help with...') in the hero — lets visitors self-qualify instantly rather than scrolling through services

plumber.name: WPJ Heating
plumber.standout: Warm orange gradient brand, Trustpilot 4.8/5 with 1007 reviews, transparent pricing page, and a refreshingly honest note warning customers to beware of providers with 'perfect' 5/5 ratings
plumber.key_element: Triple review badge row (Trustpilot + Google + Checkatrade) in the hero area — immediately answers the trust question before anything else

plumber.name: Aspect Maintenance
plumber.standout: Covers 24+ trades from one site with postcode availability checker, purple brand stands out in a sea of blue plumber sites, 24/7/365 messaging throughout
plumber.key_element: Postcode-first interaction model — visitor enters postcode before anything else, confirming coverage and creating commitment micro-step

electrician.name: Green Electrical
electrician.standout: NICEIC approved, Which? Trusted Trader 4.85/5, covers residential + EV charging + solar — a modern electrician expanding into green energy services with clean design
electrician.key_element: Service diversification layout — separate nav sections for traditional electrical, EV charging, solar PV, and battery storage show how a small electrician can position for the energy transition

electrician.name: MD Electrical
electrician.standout: Dark, professional colour scheme with hover-reveal project cards, scales from single domestic jobs to large M&E contracts, client logo wall builds commercial credibility
electrician.key_element: Project showcase cards with hover animations — each card reveals a description on hover, turning a portfolio into an interactive experience without extra pages

electrician.name: Electrician Website Design (agency example)
electrician.standout: Meta-example: a site designed specifically to sell electrician websites, so it demonstrates best practices — fast load, mobile-first, clear CTAs, local SEO patterns
electrician.key_element: Useful as a checklist of what electrician clients expect (certifications above fold, click-to-call, area pages) rather than a direct design reference

builder_construction.name: Jericho Construction
builder_construction.standout: Clean, photography-led layout with masonry grid portfolio, social enterprise story woven throughout ('55% of team on social projects'), Gas Safe + NAPIT badges
builder_construction.key_element: Social impact narrative as differentiator — the CIC status and community mission turn a standard builder site into something with genuine purpose and emotional resonance

builder_construction.name: GMI Construction Group
builder_construction.standout: Corporate-grade site with structured case studies, clear metadata and headings for SEO, project filtering by sector (residential, commercial, education)
builder_construction.key_element: Case study filtering system — while too corporate for most Nith Digital clients, the idea of filtering projects by type/sector is worth borrowing for builders with diverse portfolios

builder_construction.name: Nivo Digital (roundup reference)
builder_construction.standout: Comprehensive roundup featuring Longreach Scaffolding (full-width video hero), Jericho Construction, and GMI — useful as a pattern library of what works in UK construction web design
builder_construction.key_element: Full-width video hero (as seen on Longreach Scaffolding) — showing work in action builds more trust than static photos for construction companies

landscaper_gardener.name: Charlotte Rowe Garden Design
landscaper_gardener.standout: Asymmetric grid layout where photography is the protagonist, restrained professional language, RHS Chelsea gold medal credentials, SGLD fellowship badge, 250+ projects
landscaper_gardener.key_element: Photography-as-content approach — images occupy 60-70% of viewport with text anchoring the opposite side, letting garden work speak for itself without marketing hype

landscaper_gardener.name: Cameron Gardens
landscaper_gardener.standout: Luxury positioning with Playfair Display serif typography, earth-tone palette (#453a2d), full-screen image slider, House & Garden Top 50 and Country Life Top 100 badges
landscaper_gardener.key_element: Serif typography for luxury feel — Playfair Display headings on warm brown backgrounds create an aspirational, premium aesthetic that elevates garden design beyond 'just landscaping'

landscaper_gardener.name: The Garden Builders
landscaper_gardener.standout: 29 years heritage, RHS Chelsea + BALI + SGD award badges, before/after portfolio galleries, sustainability FAQ section
landscaper_gardener.key_element: Before/after gallery format — showing garden transformations is the most powerful trust signal for landscapers, more convincing than any testimonial

painter_decorator.name: The Good Painter
painter_decorator.standout: Warm colour palette (coral CTAs, cream/plum sections), Playfair Display serif headings with Source Sans Pro body, process transparency section, 6-month warranty, Farrow & Ball/Dulux partnerships
painter_decorator.key_element: Alternating background colour sections (cream → taupe → plum) create visual rhythm and break up content without using cards or heavy borders — elegant for service businesses

painter_decorator.name: G.D. Parvin Decorators
painter_decorator.standout: Earthy green palette, personality-driven copy ('Nice People's Favourite Decorators'), Guild of Master Craftsmen badge, 25+ years heritage, 4.95/5 rating from 100 reviews
painter_decorator.key_element: Personality-first branding — the casual, human tone ('since last century!') makes a trades business feel approachable and memorable vs generic corporate decorator sites

painter_decorator.name: Pimlico Painters and Decorators
painter_decorator.standout: Clean London-focused decorator site with project portfolio, service area pages for local SEO, Trustpilot integration, 4.1-star rating
painter_decorator.key_element: Area-specific landing pages (Chelsea, Kensington, Battersea) — essential for local SEO, each page targets a postcode area with tailored content

roofer.name: JTC Roofing
roofer.standout: Specialist zinc/metal roofing contractor, navy/teal colour scheme (#19697E), service pages organised by material type (copper, lead, aluminium, zinc), certification badges (VMZINC, CSCS, FTMRC)
roofer.key_element: Material-type navigation — organising services by roofing material rather than generic 'services' page helps specialist roofers demonstrate expertise and improves SEO for specific search terms

roofer.name: Buckstone Roofing
roofer.standout: Family-run Edinburgh roofer since 1989, Sarnafil Roof Assured + British Safety Council memberships, service pages for each roofing type, emergency repair emphasis
roofer.key_element: Emergency responsiveness messaging — testimonials specifically highlight fast response times, which is the #1 concern for roofing customers dealing with leaks or storm damage

roofer.name: Briggs Amasco
roofer.standout: UK's leading national commercial roofing company, heritage since 1865, clean corporate design — more corporate than typical Nith Digital client but demonstrates how heritage builds trust
roofer.key_element: Heritage timeline as trust anchor — '160 years of roofing' is the ultimate credibility statement; even a 5-year-old roofer can use a timeline to show growth and milestones

cleaner.name: Housekeep
cleaner.standout: 300,000+ 5-star reviews, postcode search in hero, real cleaner profiles with photos and individual reviews, Guardian/Times press logos, step-by-step process infographic
cleaner.key_element: Real staff profiles with individual reviews — showing actual cleaners with their names, photos and customer feedback builds personal trust in a way that generic 'our team' pages never can

cleaner.name: Daily Poppins
cleaner.standout: 26 years in business, 20,000+ happy customers, red accent brand (#ee413d), playful Mary Poppins brand identity ('practically perfect in every way'), franchise model with branch finder
cleaner.key_element: Memorable brand personality — the Mary Poppins reference makes the brand instantly recognisable and shareable, proving that trades/service businesses benefit from creative naming

cleaner.name: The Happy House Cleaning
cleaner.standout: Warm natural palette (golden yellow CTAs, sage green accents, dark green text), Comfortaa serif font for personality, approachable and friendly tone throughout
cleaner.key_element: Warm, non-corporate colour palette — golden yellows and sage greens feel domestic and trustworthy rather than clinical, perfect for a service that enters people's homes

beauty_hair_salon.name: Jo Hansford
beauty_hair_salon.standout: Royal Warrant holder, black-and-white minimalist design, Quicksand typeface at 34px, carousel hero with salon imagery, integrated e-commerce for hair products, Bazaar/Tatler/Glamour press logos
beauty_hair_salon.key_element: Royal Warrant / premium credential as hero anchor — any salon award or industry recognition should be the first thing visitors see, not buried in an 'about' page

beauty_hair_salon.name: Larry King Hair
beauty_hair_salon.standout: Sophisticated black/white/neutral palette, dual-purpose site (salon booking + product retail), hover effects on salon location cards, Harper's Bazaar and Evening Standard trust badges
beauty_hair_salon.key_element: Salon-retail integration — combining appointment booking with product e-commerce in one seamless experience creates multiple revenue streams and keeps customers in the brand ecosystem

beauty_hair_salon.name: TONI&GUY
beauty_hair_salon.standout: 60+ years heritage, Superbrands certification, mobile app with 2-click booking, LABEL.M product line integration, education/academy positioning alongside salon services
beauty_hair_salon.key_element: Salon finder with postcode search — for multi-location salons this is essential, but even single-location salons benefit from a prominent location/map section answering 'where are you?'

restaurant_cafe.name: Dishoom
restaurant_cafe.standout: Cultural storytelling website — warm beige palette, Cheltenham BT serif typography, poetic hero copy, 25 million meals donated (B-Corp), 11 locations, recipes section turns visitors into fans
restaurant_cafe.key_element: Narrative-first design — the site reads like a love letter to Bombay rather than a restaurant menu, creating emotional connection before the booking CTA appears

restaurant_cafe.name: Sketch London
restaurant_cafe.standout: Interactive art-forward website by studio HATO — no interior photography (deliberate surprise), gamified room experiences (grow a carpet, create a soundtrack, design food plating), minimal noir aesthetic
restaurant_cafe.key_element: Interactive micro-experiences per room — while too avant-garde for most clients, the principle of giving each service/space its own interactive identity is powerful for multi-offering restaurants

restaurant_cafe.name: Padella
restaurant_cafe.standout: Ultra-minimal design — three location cards as hero, Oswald + Roboto Condensed typography, founder story ('Tim and Jordan's dream'), honest communication about delays, no booking system (walk-in only)
restaurant_cafe.key_element: Radical simplicity — proving that a restaurant site only needs location, menu, and story; everything else is noise. Perfect reference for indie cafes and small restaurants

restaurant_cafe.name: Hawksmoor
restaurant_cafe.standout: Premium parallax sliders, gold accent (#8f7400) on black for fine-dining feel, Calligraffitti decorative font at 400% for drama, responsive mega menu, Sunday roast voted UK's best twice
restaurant_cafe.key_element: Gold-on-black colour scheme with oversized decorative typography — instantly communicates premium positioning without saying a word; adaptable for any upscale restaurant or bar

accountant.name: Mazuma
accountant.standout: Anti-corporate accountancy brand — vibrant purple (#6a0dad) with turquoise accents, illustrated character hero ('accounting for you beautiful weirdos'), Castledown custom font, BBC/Sky/Forbes press logos
accountant.key_element: Personality-driven positioning in a boring industry — proves that accountancy doesn't need to look corporate; bold colours and humour attract the exact freelancer/sole trader audience Nith Digital serves

accountant.name: Crunch
accountant.standout: Animated typing hero cycling through audience types (freelancers, contractors, creatives), gradient text effects (pink/blue/purple), interactive quote builder, Trustpilot 4.5 stars, ACCA/CIOT/ICAEW logos
accountant.key_element: Dynamic audience targeting in hero — the typing animation that cycles through 'for freelancers... for contractors... for creatives' makes every visitor feel the site is speaking directly to them

accountant.name: TaxAssist Accountants
accountant.standout: UK's largest small business accountant network (410+ offices), local branch finder, free consultation CTA, franchise model proving the demand for accessible, jargon-free accountancy
accountant.key_element: Branch finder as primary interaction — for any multi-location service business, making 'find your local office' the first action is more effective than generic service descriptions
